questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 58° and angle B measures 67°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 58° − 67° = 55°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one angle measuring 34°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: One angle is 90° (right angle), another is 34°. The third angle = 180° − 90° − 34° = 56°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the vertex angle (the angle between the two equal sides) measures 44°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. If the vertex angle is 44°, then 2x + 44 = 180, so x = 68°.

questions.question: Two angles in a triangle measure 30° and 90°. If one side adjacent to the right angle is 5 and the hypotenuse is 10, what is the length of the other side adjacent to the right angle?
questions.explanation: This is a 30-60-90 triangle. The sides are in ratio 1 : √3 : 2. With hypotenuse 10, sides are 5 and 5√3. The side opposite 30° is 5; the side opposite 60° is 5√3.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, angle P = angle Q. If angle R measures 48°, what is the measure of angle P?
questions.explanation: Triangle PQR is isosceles with P = Q. Then P + Q + 48 = 180, so 2P = 132, giving P = 66°.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle measures 115°. One of the two non-adjacent interior angles measures 42°. What is the measure of the other non-adjacent interior angle?
questions.explanation: By the exterior angle theorem, an exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So 115° = 42° + x, giving x = 73°.
